name: Cassava Bacterial Blight
symptoms: Angular water-soaked spots on leaves, blight, wilting, stem dieback and gum exudation on stems.
solution: Plant resistant varieties and clean disease-free cuttings; remove and burn infected plants; rotate crops and avoid working in the field when wet.

name: Cassava Anthracnose
symptoms: Sunken dark cankers on stems, dieback of young shoots and fruit lesions, often worse in the rainy season.
solution: Use healthy cuttings from disease-free plants; prune and destroy affected stems; improve spacing for airflow and apply a copper-based fungicide where available.

name: Cassava Mosaic
symptoms: Yellow-green mosaic mottling and distortion of leaves, stunted plants and reduced tuber yield.
solution: Plant resistant or tolerant varieties and virus-free cuttings; remove infected plants early (roguing); control the whitefly vector that spreads the virus.

name: Fusarium Wilt
symptoms: Yellowing of lower leaves, one-sided wilting, brown discoloration of the inner stem and eventual plant collapse.
solution: Use resistant varieties and clean seed; practise long crop rotation; improve drainage and solarise or treat soil; remove and destroy infected plants.

name: Banana Bacterial Wilt
symptoms: Yellowing and wilting of leaves, premature ripening and rotting of fruit, and yellow bacterial ooze from cut stems.
solution: Remove and destroy infected mats; sterilise cutting tools between plants; remove male buds with a forked stick; use clean planting material.

name: Pythium Root Rot
symptoms: Soft brown rotting of roots, damping-off of seedlings, stunted growth and wilting despite moist soil.
solution: Improve drainage and avoid overwatering; use raised beds and well-drained media; rotate crops and apply a suitable biofungicide or fungicide drench.
